$comment: Source of truth for the apodictic:persona block (Reader-Persona Simulation). A persona is a PARAMETERIZATION of the reader-experience lens by a declared reading DISPOSITION — never a character. It has disposition axes, NOT a name, a job, or a life. Each axis is a top-level string enum (so the subset engine can validate it); `target` (boolean) marks the one persona the book is for. The guarantee that a persona is a disposition vector and not an invented character is enforced by the validator's CLOSED-KEY check (D5, a non-overridable ERROR — the subset engine silently allows unknown properties, so 'the schema has no biography field' would be a FALSE guarantee). `thematic_receptivity` reuses the Argument Engine's Audience.Receptivity enum VERBATIM (SYMPATHETIC | MIXED | HOSTILE). This is one of the three mechanical guards (with D2 grounded-prediction and D4 anti-fabrication) that keep persona simulation a LENS and not the non-viable Simulated Reader Focus Group (Horizon item 17). Validated by scripts/persona_divergence.py. See docs/reader-persona-simulation.md.
title: APODICTIC reader persona (apodictic.persona.v1)
